category_title: Children who hardly speak or do not speak at all
minimal_context: The student and therapist are looking at toys during a picture-naming exercise to describe what is happening.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Look at this picture of the family with the toys. Tell me what is happening here.

STUDENT: Papa.

TUTOR: Okay, what else?

STUDENT: Ja. (Uses a deictic gesture to point at another toy in the picture)

TUTOR: Can you tell me more about that?

STUDENT: Nee. (Makes an iconic hand movement without speech, relying on a one-word sentence because they cannot verbally express the complex concept they have in mind)

options.A: Respond to the student by saying, "Another one already? No way! I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going with the picture-naming exercise."
options.B: Put the student in a position where they are less distracted, so they are not seeing other toys in the therapy room during the exercise.
options.C: Use a visual chart for checking in where specific numbers, colors, and faces related to anxiety are matched with feeling words during the session.
options.D: Train the use of signs more explicitly by offering topic-related signs during the picture-naming exercise or by supporting specific syntactic elements for sentence production.
